title: Temp Programmes & Partnerships Executive
description:
The employee will support both outreach/volunteer management and programme-related functions. This includes assisting in planning and implementing inclusive activities for persons with disabilities (PWD), helping to coordinate volunteers, and engaging community partners to strengthen support for SUN-DAC’s clients. Interns will work closely with staff, volunteers, and clients to create a safe, engaging, and supportive environment that promotes personal growth, community participation, and overall well-being.

Key Responsibilities

1. Volunteer Recruitment and Management

  • Assist to develop and execute a volunteer recruitment plan to attract a diverse pool of volunteers.
  • Conduct training, orientation, and ongoing support for volunteers to prepare them to assist clients effectively.
  • Create and manage volunteer schedules

2. Program Coordination

  • Collaborate with the program team to identify volunteer needs for specific activities and events.
  • Assist to organise and coordinate special events, workshops, and activities that promote community engagement and support for individuals with disabilities.

3. Communication

  • Draft marketing materials, newsletters, and social medial content to highlight the centre's activities and volunteer opportunities.
  • Maintain clear and open communication with volunteers, staff, and community partners to foster a collaborative environment.

4. Administrative Duties (Programme)

  • Assist in budgeting and resource management for program activities, ensuring efficient use of funds and materials.

5. Community Engagement

  • Participate in community outreach initiatives to promote awareness of the centre's programs and services.
  • When possible, engage with families and caregivers to provide information and resources related to the centre's offerings.

6. Program Implementation

  • Support the execution of programs and activities, ensuring they are conducted smoothly and effectively.
  • Coordinate logistics for program activities, including materials, scheduling, and resource allocation.

Job Requirements

  • Communication Skills: Good verbal and written communication skills to interact effectively with clients, families, and stakeholders.
  • Organisational Skills: Good organisational abilities to manage multiple programs and activities simultaneously.
  • Teamwork: Ability to work collaboratively with team members, volunteers, and community partners.
  • Problem-Solving Skills: Creative thinking and adaptability to address challenges in dynamic environments.
  • Knowledge in Program Development: Knowledge of program design, implementation, and evaluation processes, particularly in a community setting.
  • Computer Proficiency: Proficient in using Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and experience with data management systems.

postedCompany.description:
SUN-DAC is a non-profit social service agency affiliated with the National Council of Social Service. It serves and cares for persons with disabilities in 3 Day Activities Centres (Upper Thomson, Choa Chua Kang & Bedok South). Most of our clients are persons with moderate to severe disabilities.

SUN-DAC mission is to help people with disabilities to live each day positively with tailored programmes of constructive activities. It also provides respite for parents and caregivers of people with disability through support and assistance services.
postedCompany.name: SUN-DAC
